basics.name: Torsten Uhlmann
basics.label: Senior Full Stack Software Engineer & Consultant
basics.summary:
Senior Full Stack Software Engineer with nearly 30 years of experience delivering robust, high-performance software solutions across diverse industries. Operating through AGYNAMIX, I specialize in taking complex, system-critical applications from prototype to production---whether leading development efforts or integrating into distributed teams, bridging intricate backend architectures with modern, responsive frontend frameworks.
I love learning new things and meeting new people. Over the years I have worked with a plethora of different programming languages---from Cobol, C++, and Java through Scala and Clojure to Rust and TypeScript. Technologies change, developer roles change, but the desire to create something useful always stays strong.
I authored the book "Lift Web Applications How-To" (Packt Publishing, 2012) and regularly contribute to open-source projects on GitHub. I am particularly good at integrating into teams and getting myself deeply involved in the subject matter.

work.name: TXODDS, Ltd.
work.summary: Developed multiple frontend applications for the TX-Scores professional scouting network---a platform connecting the sports betting ecosystem to ultra-low latency sports odds delivery.
highlights: Built several frontend applications for the tx-scores project using VueJS 3 with TypeScript.
TX-Scores is a professional scouting network---not casual coverage. A community of scouts and analysts with real-time dedicated analyst oversight for every game.
Docker-containerized deployment pipeline.

work.name: iCombine UG
work.summary: Led the migration of an expert talent-pool and project-matching platform from AngularJS to React, using a bottom-up approach that combined both component types during the transition.
highlights: Architected and implemented bottom-up migration strategy from AngularJS to React, enabling co-existence of both component types during the transition.
Worked with Scala and the Lift web framework for backend services.
Platform connects experts to the right project roles, trainings, and peers via skills, interest, and availability matching.

work.name: Scaled Agile, Inc.
work.summary: Key role in developing and modernizing an online planning platform for remote learning, PI planning, and collaborative events. Five-year engagement with significant product growth.
highlights: Expanded Collaborate's reach by integrating collaborative planning features such as remote PI planning and online training classes.
Backend built with PostgreSQL, Scala, and Lift; containerized and deployed on AWS.
Frontend migrated from legacy AngularJS 1 to Angular 12 / TypeScript within an NX workspace.
Designed a push-based data warehouse integration.

work.name: Ascendant, Inc.
work.summary: Senior engineer and mentor in a small team of high-profile developers building a multi-tenant SaaS compliance and risk management suite. Balanced industry-specific compliance requirements with engineering problem-solving over a 5.5-year engagement.
highlights: Implemented state-of-the-art frontend with AngularJS and MarkoJS; backend with Scala, Lift, and MongoDB.
Mentored junior developers while furthering expertise in financial services, asset classes, and trading patterns from a compliance perspective.
Some services implemented in Go.

work.name: Twisp, Inc.
work.summary: Developed a Lift-based personalized information engine application.
highlights: Built a multi-user Scala/Lift web application using MySQL, Apache Shiro, and Amazon Cloudsearch.

work.name: Underscore Consulting
work.summary: Developed part of a Scala/Lift client application for an innovative real-time interactive NFC platform (TagTrail) for a US corporation. Delivered a rich reporting tool as an international (D, UK, AU) team.
highlights: Took responsibility for---and delivered---a rich reporting tool within a cloud-based NFC/QR-code marketing campaign tracking platform.
Collaborated in a small international team (Germany, UK, Australia).

work.name: Sgrouples, Inc. (now MeWe.com)
work.summary: Implemented system-critical features and managed AWS deployment for a secure, group-based privacy-conscious social communication platform.
highlights: Independently implemented many business-critical features of the application, managed deployment on AWS servers, performed MongoDB migrations and maintenance.
Built frontend JavaScript/CSS/HTML5 components using jQuery alongside Scala/Lift backend development.
Significantly contributed to evolving the application from prototype to production readiness within a few months.
Worked in an internationally distributed remote team; communication in English.

work.name: T-Systems
work.summary: 15-year engagement spanning multiple major projects at T-Systems (formerly T-Systems GEI GmbH and T-Systems SL SI), progressing from COBOL conversion work to leading sub-projects and building high-performance enterprise applications.
highlights: Maintained and extended a mid-sized Eclipse RCP application for the in-house test department (EMF, Teneo/Hibernate, Oracle, JDBC).
Extended a data transformation framework for mass-data migration (HP-Tandem NonStop, Oracle 9i); classifiable as EAI/EII system.
Created an Eclipse RCP DSL application using the XText framework, enabling non-developers to write business rules.
Built and extended a Perl-based distributed test framework for cross-machine automated system testing.
Designed and developed a high-performance data extraction application processing millions of records from multiple Oracle schemas; multi-threaded for throughput.

Developed invoice generation system using LaTeX with email delivery (optional encryption/signing); scalable multi-process C++ backend communicating via CORBA with an Oracle database and Java/J2EE frontend.
Developed an Online Payment Platform (Santorin) using J2EE on BEA WebLogic and Portal with Oracle; and a CORBA bridge linking a legacy server application to a Tomcat server farm.
Created COBOL converters for customer invoice data targeting AFP printers; developed Java GUI applications for invoice layout specification.
Sub-project lead from August 1999.

work.name: 1822direkt Sparkasse Frankfurt
work.summary: Developed a JSF frontend for managing and creating customer correspondence at a German bank. Letters were generated template-based via a LaTeX backend and delivered as PDF or sent to a high-volume printer.
highlights: JSF 1.2 with MyFaces on Tomcat 5 and JBoss.
LaTeX-based document generation pipeline for customer printables.

work.name: G & K Datensysteme GmbH
work.summary: Developed drivers and associated software for retail cash registers, scanners, and printers used with the client's point-of-sale software. Also contributed to development of the retail cash register software itself.
highlights: Driver development for cash registers, scanners, and printers.
Contributed to the core POS application.

work.name: Leicher GmbH
work.summary: Developed converter software for converting data between the Datanorm industry standard and the client's in-house software standard.

work.name: Jahn Büroorganisation
work.summary: Developed management software for tracking printers and copiers leased by the client to its customers.

publications.name: Lift Web Applications How-To
publications.summary: Practical guide to building web applications with Lift, the Scala web framework. Published by Packt Publishing.

projects.name: AGYNAMIX Invoicer
projects.description: Modern desktop invoicing application for freelancers and small businesses in the DACH market. Fully GoBD-compliant with ZUGFeRD and XRechnung e-invoice support.
highlights: Cross-platform (Windows, macOS, Linux) with a modern Jetpack Compose UI.
Features: customizable invoice templates, automatic numbering, VAT calculations, payment tracking, customer management, and reporting.
Offline-capable with local data storage---no cloud dependency. Emphasizes data sovereignty and financial management for professionals.

projects.name: Symnix -- Cloud Provisioning Platform
projects.description: Co-founded web application with a high-performance Rust / PostgreSQL backend for cloud server setup and provisioning.
highlights: Allows individuals and organizations to create, manage, and monitor multiple hosts with multiple installed applications.
Streamlines server management using latest industry standards and best practices; Docker-containerized deployment.

projects.name: AGYNAMIX Numerix
projects.description: Multi-tenant tool for freelancers and small companies with modules for time tracking, invoice generation, document management, knowledge base, chat rooms, calendar, and user management.
highlights: Invoice generation from tracked time; PDF output via Flying Saucer.
Role-based permissions system similar to Apache Shiro's wildcard permissions.
Multi-document upload and knowledge base with notifications.

projects.name: Unser Gartenverein (Garden Community Manager)
projects.description: Hosted web application for managing German garden communities (allotment associations): tenant management, meter readings, invoicing, document management, and shared calendar.
highlights: Manages water and power meter readings collection and invoicing.
Dashboard overview with quick navigation to all areas.

projects.name: AGYNAMIX Simidude
projects.description: Cross-platform network clipboard and file sharing tool. Automatically discovers other instances on the local network and enables drag-and-drop file transfer and clipboard synchronization across machines.
highlights: Runs on Windows, Linux, macOS, and in virtual machines.
Automatic LAN discovery; drag-and-drop file/directory transfer between connected computers.
Available as 30-day trial with online payment.

projects.name: AGYNAMIX Net-Herald
projects.description: First AGYNAMIX product---monitoring software for water supply companies. Server-side Java application with Eclipse RCP GUI for real-time data visualization.
highlights: Server: Java 5, JMX, Spring, PostgreSQL, ESB bus architecture for component decoupling.
GSM modem connectivity for SMS alerts; OPC (OLE for Process Control) library for connecting to existing industrial software.
Eclipse RCP client using GEF for charts and real-time data visualization.
Led a dedicated team to bring the product to market.

references.name: Neil Visnapuu — Managing Director, Ascendant Compliance Management
references.reference: Torsten worked with our team at Ascendant for roughly six years. He was one of our earliest developers and served as a senior engineer and mentor to junior developers. He thrived in our distributed, virtual environment and handled the pressures related to a multi-tenant SaaS platform with grace. Were it not for our acquisition by private equity and shifting capital expenditures, he would remain with us indefinitely, as I would want.

references.name: Richard Dallaway — Partner, Underscore Consulting LLP
references.reference: He brought technical knowledge, especially with Lift, Scala, and Angular. He worked with the customer to understand their needs. As well as generally contributing to the project, he took on responsibility for (and delivered) a rich reporting tool.

references.name: Jonathan Wolfe, Ph.D. — Chief Scientist, Sgrouples.com
references.reference: It has been my greatest pleasure working with Torsten. He has proven himself capable of taking on any task or challenge given to him. He is an excellent Scala developer, an expert in the Lift framework, and has demonstrated mastery of several types of databases. He is a committed, hard-working, resourceful programmer, a faithful team player, and a true pleasure to work with. Whatever future teams or projects he joins will be lucky indeed.

references.name: Project Center Team Leader — T-Systems GEI GmbH
references.reference: The freelancer has a very broad spectrum of technological knowledge. As architect and developer he worked confidently with object-oriented programming concepts. All work was completed to our fullest satisfaction on time. Beyond the concrete programming tasks he showed very high engagement. He identified errors and improvement potential in requirements and implementation. He supported his colleagues beyond his own area of responsibility. Working with him was very pleasant. He was a treasure for the project team.

references.name: Project Leader — T-Systems
references.reference: The consultant is proficient in C++ and Java; he works confidently with object-oriented programming concepts. All work was completed to our fullest satisfaction on time. I would very much like to work with him again.
